macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Major US Stock Indexes Record Weekly Declines Amid Sell Signals
items.summary: The Dow Jones fell 3% (1,476 points), S&P 500 dropped 2.02% (139 points), Nasdaq declined 1.27% (317 points), and Russell 2000 sank 2.33% for the week ending March 6, 2026, with all major indexes breaking key trend lines.
items.source: YouTube - The 11th Hour With Stephanie Ruhle / askSlim Market Week

items.title: Oil Prices Surge 35% Sparking Fears of Potential Black Monday Crash
items.summary: Oil prices have surged 35%, raising concerns of a Black Monday stock market crash similar to historical events, as markets show all sell signals and indexes like Dow, S&P, and Nasdaq face rejection at moving averages.
items.source: YouTube - Do We See a Black Monday CRASH Next?

items.title: Markets Can Handle Temporary Oil Spike But Not Prolonged Rise
items.summary: Crossmark Global Investments CEO Bob Doll states that financial markets can absorb a short-term oil price spike but warn of risks if it becomes prolonged amid global tensions.

items.title: Serious Global Events Sometimes Fail to Impact Stock Markets
items.summary: Barron’s Roundtable panelist Jack Hough notes that even deadly serious world events do not always affect market performance, urging investors to keep key factors in mind.

items.title: Microsoft Faces Bearish Earnings Reaction in Weakening Tech Sector
items.summary: Microsoft experienced a bearish post-earnings move, holding at support amid broader tech declines, with Nasdaq 100 rejected at the 20-week moving average and S&P 500 getting 'smoked'.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Trades Above $70,000 Amid Middle East Tensions and Inflation Concerns
items.summary: Bitcoin traded just above $70,000 as Middle East tensions drove oil higher and traders reassessed inflation ahead of the U.S. jobs report. Technical analysts noted support levels around $62.5K-$63K and resistance at $69K-$69.5K.

items.title: Fed, OCC & FDIC Approve Equal Capital Treatment for Tokenized Securities
items.summary: A joint statement from the Federal Reserve, OCC, and FDIC confirmed that tokenized securities receive the same capital treatment as traditional assets, with no extra penalties for banks. This regulatory clarity is expected to enable major financial institutions like JP Morgan and Goldman Sachs to integrate blockchain technology.

items.title: XRP Experiences Significant Exchange Outflows and Whale Accumulation
items.summary: 7 billion XRP left exchanges in February, with 3.38 billion from Binance alone, creating a supply vacuum. Whale wallets now hold 83% of XRP supply, with analysts predicting potential price targets of $2.50-$4 by late 2026.

items.title: Ethereum and Altcoins Show Overbought Signals on 4-Hour Timeframes
items.summary: Ethereum is rejecting from key resistance levels while Solana continues to display overbought signals. Chain Link shows strong support at $7.90-$8.50 and resistance at $9.50-$10, with RSI indicators suggesting potential short-term cooling off.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Scientists Propose Quantum Computers Could Generate Training Data for AI Chemistry Models
items.summary: Researchers from IonQ and Microsoft suggest using quantum computers to generate highly accurate data for training AI models to simulate chemical systems more efficiently, combining quantum precision with AI speed for faster materials and drug discovery.

items.title: AI Technology Trends: Emerging Patterns in Machine Learning and Applications
items.summary: Analysis of 548 articles reveals three major clusters: technical AI development (194 articles), AI applications in tech and media (228 articles), and AI solutions for renewable energy (126 articles), with growing focus on ethical AI and human-AI collaboration.

items.title: China Prioritizes Open-Source AI as Competitive Strategy in Five-Year Plan
items.summary: China's latest five-year plan emphasizes open-source AI development and domestic innovation as key competitive advantages, with specific focus on companies like DeepSeek alongside investments in quantum computing, 6G, and humanoid robots.

items.title: AI Cited in 8% of Job Cuts So Far in 2026, But Analysts Say Impact Overstated
items.summary: While AI was explicitly cited in 12,304 job cut announcements (8% of total cuts) through early 2026, analysts argue AI's role in recent unemployment increases is minimal compared to broader economic factors.

items.title: Oracle Reportedly Planning Thousands of Job Cuts as AI Data Center Investments Strain Finances
items.summary: Oracle is expected to announce significant layoffs in its cloud division this month, with positions to be backfilled by AI, as the company raises $50 billion through debt and equity sales to fund AI data center infrastructure.

items.title: 70% of Healthcare Organizations Now Using AI, According to NVIDIA Survey
items.summary: New data shows widespread AI adoption in the healthcare sector, with 70% of organizations implementing AI solutions, while insider breaches in healthcare cost 48% more than in other industries.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Resident Evil Requiem Emerges as Critical Standout with 89 Metacritic Score
items.summary: Capcom's latest horror entry blends investigative horror with action sequences, delivering on atmosphere and cinematic storytelling. The game has become one of 2026's best releases so far.

items.title: Slay the Spire 2 Surpasses Marathon as Top-Selling Title on Steam
items.summary: The sequel has climbed Steam's sales charts while developers confirm the game will feature no microtransactions.

items.title: Crimson Desert Marketing Director Addresses Console Performance Concerns
items.summary: Pearl Abyss marketing director Will Powers responded to accusations about withholding console footage, arguing that demanding information on specific timelines constitutes entitlement.

items.title: Helldivers 2 File Size Drops to 23GB Following Legacy Build Removal
items.summary: The game will see a significant size reduction on March 17 when legacy builds are removed from the platform.

items.title: Overwatch Teases NieR: Automata Crossover Skins
items.summary: Blizzard continues live-service collaborations with a new crossover featuring NieR: Automata characters.

items.title: Ubisoft Announces Assassin's Creed IV: Black Flag Remake
items.summary: Ubisoft has teased a remake of the popular pirate-themed entry in the Assassin's Creed franchise.

items.title: Nioh 3 Launches with Expanded Combat Systems and Larger World
items.summary: Team Ninja's samurai fantasy series returns with an 86 Metacritic score, featuring enhanced swordplay mechanics and more flexible character builds.

items.title: Replaced 2.5D Platformer Set for April 14 Release
items.summary: Sad Cat Studios' dynamic platformer features AAA-quality level design and environments, arriving on multiple platforms.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
